steps.title: Locate the clause boundary
steps.content: The first independent clause ends after resource conservation. The word though is supplementary, and the next independent clause begins with efficiency gains. The punctuation must set off though and separate the two clauses.

steps.title: Reject comma splices
steps.content: A and D lack the semicolon needed before the second independent clause, so both create comma-splice problems there. B places the semicolon before though instead of setting off the supplementary word with a comma. The answer is C.
